Publication Title: 2024 Asia-Pacific Conference on Software Engineering, Social Network Analysis and Intelligent Computing (SSAIC)

Título: A Push-Pull Train for Safety of Autonomous Vehicle Software
Autor: S. A. Wadekar
Abstract: A new software engineering framework is provided for assessing and enhancing the safety of artificial-intelligence (AI) based software for controlling autonomous vehicles. The new framework is based on specifying and sharing datasets used for training AI-based systems.
Publication Title: 2023 15th International Conference on COMmunication Systems & NETworkS (COMSNETS)

Título: Taking Engineering Automation to the Next Level with Artificial Intelligence
Autor: P. Barar; K. K. Gan; J. Lee
Abstract: The evolution in manufacturing automation has helped manufacturers across all industries to become more efficient and profitable. Today, it is expected that many operations within a modern production plant to include some level of automation to drive production efficiency and to reduce human errors. As we step into the era of smart manufacturing, this expectation will continue to grow, not only in the scope of automation, but also its sophistication. With key enabling technologies such as Artificial Intelligence (AI), engineering applications on the factory floor are becoming more intelligent and autonomous. This paper highlights examples in the semiconductor manufacturing industry on how AI can further push the envelope in automation allowing manufacturers to achieve results faster and be even more efficient in process control.
Publication Title: 2020 International Symposium on Semiconductor Manufacturing (ISSM)

Título: Early warning system prototype development to mitigate coastal aquaculture activities from the climate variability using internet of things (IoT) and artificial intelligence (AI) technologies in Thailand
Autor: P. Suvanachai; A. Kawbunjun; J. Kittiwanich; N. Wutticharoenmongkol; N. Poolsawat; N. Somwadee; W. Pophan; P. Jamying; K. Hornak; C. Yoakarakun; A. Chucherd; A. Intarasab; M. Panchapornudomlap
Abstract: According to Department of Fisheries (Thailand), financial aid for farmers impacted by disaster between 2019 and 2021 showed that Nakhon Si Thammarat province and Surat Thani province in Southern Thailand obtained the most compensation, with an annual budget exceeding 5 million USD. In 2021, 6,074 farmers in Nakhon Si Thammarat province and 1,147 in Surat Thani province received financial assistance due to climate change-related disasters, which they could neither predict nor prevent in time. This research developed a prototype early warning system using IoT technology to connect devices, exchange data, and measure water quality parameters affecting cultured aquatic animals. AI processes this data to predict water quality conditions and provide early warnings via a web application and SMS, assisting targeted farmers to avoid risks and mitigate damages. The results showed that environmental conditions affecting aquatic species could be forecasted within 72 hours in advance by using a decision tree model with 91.22% accuracy. Additionally, 120 farmers and relevant stakeholders participated in the project. To further assess the system’s effectiveness beyond simulation and controlled testing, it was deployed in estuarine and marine environments in Thailand, where highly variable environmental conditions pose significant real-world challenges.
Publication Title: 2025 IEEE/ACIS 29th International Conference on Software Engineering, Artificial Intelligence, Networking and Parallel/Distributed Computing (SNPD)

Título: Designing a Reverse Engineering Tool for Functional Requirements Prioritization
Autor: O. Alharbi; A. Alrashed; S. Albogobar
Abstract: This study examines the role of reverse engineering in developing functional requirements, with a focus on overcoming current challenges and proposing solutions to improve the adaptability and efficiency of software development projects. Through a mixed-methods approach, the research combines quantitative data from a survey of reverse software engineering professionals with qualitative insights from interviews. The study identifies significant challenges related to standardization, documentation, and stakeholder alignment, offering practical recommendations to address these gaps and enhance project outcomes.
Publication Title: 2025 IEEE 5th International Conference on Software Engineering and Artificial Intelligence (SEAI)

Título: A New Approach for Machine Learning Security Risk Assessment – Work in Progress
Autor: J. Yajima; M. Inui; T. Oikawa; F. Kasahara; I. Morikawa; N. Yoshioka
Abstract: We propose a new security risk assessment approach for Machine Learning-based AI systems (ML systems). The assessment of security risks of ML systems requires expertise in ML security. So, ML system developers, who may not know much about ML security, cannot assess the security risks of their systems. By using our approach, a ML system developers can easily assess the security risks of the ML system. In performing the assessment, the ML system developer only has to answer the yes/no questions about the specification of the ML system. In our trial, we confirmed that our approach works correctly. CCS CONCEPTS • Security and privacy; • Computing methodologies → Artificial intelligence; Machine learning;
Publication Title: 2022 IEEE/ACM 1st International Conference on AI Engineering – Software Engineering for AI (CAIN)

Título: Trustworthy AI: Industry-Guided Tooling of the Methods
Autor: Z. Chihani
Abstract: The need to assess and validate the trustworthiness of AI (robustness, transparency, safety, security, etc.,) has been the subject of considerable academic work for some time now. A natural evolution of such research efforts is to have a tangible impact in the industrial sector and in the upcoming standards. To this end, theoretical feasibility of algorithmic methods is not enough: one needs to put these methods inside usable tools that can scale to real-world problems. Evidently, this need has not gone unnoticed either and several teams are actively working on maturing their tools further and further in a constant race with a very rapidly moving field. While fundamental research is a paramount bedrock, in the present communication, we want to focus on how far we have come in satisfying the goal of seeing AI safely permeating our future. To this end, we will give a brief overview of recent collaborations with industrial actors in an effort to give the reader a wider notion of trustworthiness, one that may come into play on their own use-cases.
Publication Title: 2024 IEEE/ACM 3rd International Conference on AI Engineering – Software Engineering for AI (CAIN)

Título: Integrating IoT and AI Technologies in Engineering Education: A Smart Home Control Case Study
Autor: R. Laszlo; R. Holonec; L. Grindei; B. Adrian; N. András
Abstract: In the rapidly evolving landscape of engineering education, bridging the gap between emerging technologies and practical applications is critical to shaping future professionals. This paper presents a comprehensive case study on integrating Internet of Things (IoT), machine learning, and advanced control mechanisms into smart home systems, as part of an innovative educational project. The study emphasizes the development and implementation of a 3D-printed smart home model controlled via hand gestures, voice commands, and manual inputs, proving a hands-on approach to combining theoretical knowledge with real-world applications.Through this project, students gain exposure to innovative technologies such as neural networks, gesture recognition algorithms, and voice command processing, offering insights into their practical deployment in IoT ecosystems. The method underscores the importance of interdisciplinary learning by merging concepts from electrical engineering, artificial intelligence, and software development.This initiative highlights the transformative impact of incorporating smart home technologies into engineering education, fostering an environment where students can bridge theory and application. By engaging in emerging fields such as IoT and artificial intelligence, learners develop skills essential for addressing modern engineering challenges. Additionally, the project serves as a platform for exploring the intersection of accessibility, automation, and sustainability, aligning education with real-world technological demands. The results underline the importance of integrating user-centric design principles in engineering curricula, preparing students to innovate and lead in a rapidly evolving technological landscape.
Publication Title: 2025 34th Annual Conference of the European Association for Education in Electrical and Information Engineering (EAEEIE)

Título: AI Ethics: An Empirical Study on the Views of Practitioners and Lawmakers
Autor: A. A. Khan; M. A. Akbar; M. Fahmideh; P. Liang; M. Waseem; A. Ahmad; M. Niazi; P. Abrahamsson
Abstract: Artificial intelligence (AI) solutions and technologies are being increasingly adopted in smart systems contexts; however, such technologies are concerned with ethical uncertainties. Various guidelines, principles, and regulatory frameworks are designed to ensure that AI technologies adhere to ethical well-being. However, the implications of AI ethics principles and guidelines are still being debated. To further explore the significance of AI ethics principles and relevant challenges, we conducted a survey of 99 randomly selected representative AI practitioners and lawmakers (e.g., AI engineers and lawyers) from 20 countries across five continents. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first empirical study that unveils the perceptions of two different types of population (AI practitioners and lawmakers) and the study findings confirm that transparency, accountability, and privacy are the most critical AI ethics principles. On the other hand, lack of ethical knowledge, no legal frameworks, and lacking monitoring bodies are found to be the most common AI ethics challenges. The impact analysis of the challenges across principles reveals that conflict in practice is a highly severe challenge. Moreover, the perceptions of practitioners and lawmakers are statistically correlated with significant differences for particular principles (e.g. fairness and freedom) and challenges (e.g. lacking monitoring bodies and machine distortion). Our findings stimulate further research, particularly empowering existing capability maturity models to support ethics-aware AI systems’ development and quality assessment.
Publication Title: IEEE Transactions on Computational Social Systems

Título: Conceptualizing the Secure Machine Learning Operations (SecMLOps) Paradigm
Autor: X. Zhang; J. Jaskolka
Abstract: Due to the proliferation of machine learning in various domains and applications, Machine Learning Operations (MLOps) was created to improve efficiency and adaptability by automating and operationalizing ML products. Because many machine learning application domains demand high levels of assurance, security has become a top priority and necessity to be involved at the beginning of ML system design. To provide theoretical guidance, we first introduce the Secure Machine Learning Operations (SecMLOps) paradigm, which extends MLOps with security considerations. We use the People, Processes, Technology, Governance and Compliance (PPTGC) framework to conceptualize SecMLOps, and to discuss challenges in adopting SecMLOps in practice. Since ML systems are often multi-concerned, analysis on how the adoption of SecMLOps impacts other system qualities, such as fairness, explainability, reliability, safety, and sustainability are provided. This paper aims to provide guidance and a research roadmap for ML researchers and organizational-level practitioners towards secure, reliable, and trustworthy MLOps.
Publication Title: 2022 IEEE 22nd International Conference on Software Quality, Reliability and Security (QRS)

Título: Linnaeus: A highly reusable and adaptable ML based log classification pipeline
Autor: A. Catovic; C. Cartwright; Y. T. Gebreyesus; S. Ferlin
Abstract: Logs are a common way to record detailed run-time information in software. As modern software systems evolve in scale and complexity, logs have become indispensable to understanding the internal states of the system. At the same time however, manually inspecting logs has become impractical. In recent times, there has been more emphasis on statistical and machine learning (ML) based methods for analyzing logs. While the results have shown promise, most of the literature focuses on algorithms and state-of-the-art (SOTA), while largely ignoring the practical aspects. In this paper we demonstrate our end-to-end log classification pipeline, Linnaeus. Besides showing the more traditional ML flow, we also demonstrate our solutions for adaptability and re-use, integration towards large scale software development processes, and how we cope with lack of labelled data. We hope Linnaeus can serve as a blueprint for, and inspire the integration of, various ML based solutions in other large scale industrial settings.
Publication Title: 2021 IEEE/ACM 1st Workshop on AI Engineering - Software Engineering for AI (WAIN)

Título: Challenges in Adopting Artificial Intelligence Based User Input Verification Framework in Reporting Software Systems
Autor: D. J. Kim; S. Locke; T. -H. P. Chen; A. Toma; S. Sporea; L. Weinkam; S. Sajedi
Abstract: Artificial intelligence is driving new industrial solutions for challenging problems once considered impossible. Many large-scale companies use AI to identify opportunities to improve business processes and products. Despite the promise and perils of AI, many traditional software systems (e.g., taxation or reporting) are implemented without AI in mind. Adopting AI-based capabilities in such software can be challenging due to a lack of resources and uncertainties in requirements. This paper documents our experience working with our industry partner on adopting AI capabilities in enterprise software. The enterprise software receives and processes thousands of user inputs with different configuration settings daily, which makes manual user input verification infeasible. To assist our industry partner, we design and integrate an AI-based input verification framework into the software. However, during the design and integration of the framework, we encounter many challenges that range from the requirement engineering process to the development, adoption, and verification process. We discuss the challenges we encountered and their corresponding solutions while working with our industrial partner to integrate the AI-based input verification framework into their non-AI software. Our experience report may provide valuable insight to practitioners and researchers on better integrating AI-based capabilities with existing software systems.
Publication Title: 2023 IEEE/ACM 45th International Conference on Software Engineering: Software Engineering in Practice (ICSE-SEIP)

Título: Integration of Machine Learning and Artificial Intelligence into IoT Systems for Intelligent Resource Management
Autor: H. Majed; H. Zorgati; R. B. Djemaa
Abstract: The Internet of Things (IoT) is revolutionizing how we interact with our environment by integrating sensors and smart devices to enable real-time data collection. A critical aspect of this technology is situation detection, which involves capturing and analyzing sensor data to understand conditions and events in real time, providing actionable insights for informed decision-making. This capability is pivotal in transforming raw data into valuable knowledge, enabling efficient monitoring and resource management. To enhance IoT system performance, advanced situation detection algorithms, seamless device communication, and rapid response mechanisms are essential. Furthermore, integrating artificial intelligence (AI) and machine learning (ML) strengthens detection capabilities, allowing systems to continuously adapt and learn. By optimizing resources through accurate, real-time situation detection, IoT improves operational efficiency, reduces costs, and minimizes environmental impacts. In this paper, we highlight the transformative role of real-time situation detection in IoT-enabled services, where machine learning (ML) and artificial intelligence (AI) serve as cornerstones for intelligent automation. By leveraging AI/ML, IoT systems can not only detect but also predict and contextualize complex events—from industrial equipment failures to environmental hazards—enabling proactive interventions. This capability is critical for applications demanding high reliability, such as smart cities, healthcare monitoring, and precision agriculture, where delays or inaccuracies in detection can lead to significant risks or resource waste. We further outline emerging challenges and future directions, advocating for explainable AI (XAI) and energy-efficient algorithms to bridge gaps between theoretical advances and real-world deployment.
Publication Title: 2025 IEEE/ACIS 29th International Conference on Software Engineering, Artificial Intelligence, Networking and Parallel/Distributed Computing (SNPD)

Título: TrustOps: A risk-based AI engineering process
Autor: J. H. Kwak
Abstract: We live in an era of artificial intelligence (AI) technology, which enables traditional HW/SW systems to help humans make wise decisions and even operate in intelligent ways. However, due to its inductive behaviors, this data-driven technology creates problems differ from those of conventional deductive systems. Therefore, the more AI is used in a wider range of industries and societies, the more problems and issues unseen before arise in human society. That's why most international organizations and nations released principles, and are struggling to find a technical methodology for assuring trustworthiness of AI. In many studies and papers, technical requirements are being presented individually and risk management is raised as the one of those. This paper intends to present an integrated and systematic engineering process that implements technical requirements in aspect of risk management.
Publication Title: 2022 13th International Conference on Information and Communication Technology Convergence (ICTC)

Título: Kidney Disease Prediction Using Multiple LSTM Neural Networks
Autor: M. A. Kamal; F. M. Ramo
Abstract: Over the last few years, artificial intelligence in healthcare has transformed everything regarding disease prediction and diagnosis. Artificial intelligence has proven useful in analyzing medical data and patient symptoms. Likewise, as this paper shows, artificial intelligence has been extremely accurate and effective at predicting kidney diseases, which are the leading causes of chronic kidney disease. Long-short-term memory was applied and implemented in this paper on a created dataset collected and organized manually from three local laboratories in Iraq. Five models were evaluated, including (long short-term memory, bidirectional long short-term memory, gated recurrent units, a combination of long short-term memory and bidirectional long short-term memory, and a hybrid model incorporating gated recurrent units and long short-term memory). It can carry out that the highest accuracy of 98% obtained from bidirectional long short-term memory, and the binary cross entropy loss is about 0.089.
Publication Title: 2025 International Conference on Computer Science and Software Engineering (CSASE)

Título: A Feature-Based Machine Learning Approach for Mixed-Criticality Systems
Autor: N. V. Varghese; A. Azim; Q. H. Mahmoud
Abstract: Driven by the recent technological advancements in the field of artificial intelligence, machine learning has emerged as a promising representation learning and decision-making method in many technological domains. Inspired by impressive these results, now machine learning techniques are also being applied to address the decision-making and control problems in the area of cyber-physical systems. For instance, some of these systems fall under the category of safety-critical systems such as chemical plants, autonomous vehicles, surgical robots, and modern medical equipment. One of the major performance issues related to the applicability of machine learning with safety-critical systems is related to the probability-based prediction nature of machine learning components used within such systems. This particular characteristic of machine learning makes it extremely difficult to guarantee safety as directed by standards such as ISO 26262. More importantly, the non-transparent and complex nature of machine learning algorithms make both the reasoning as well as formally establishing the safety aspects of the underlying system extremely difficult. The objective of this research work is to investigate on this key issue, and further on propose an efficient machine learning methodology based on the mixed-criticality approach feasible to safety-critical systems.
Publication Title: 2021 22nd IEEE International Conference on Industrial Technology (ICIT)

Título: European Perceptions of Artificial Intelligence and Their Social Variability. An Exploratory Study
Autor: A. -M. Budeanu; D. Ţurcanu; D. Rosner
Abstract: We are currently living in societies that are profoundly concerned about the impact of current and potential technologies on our present and future lives. How is the future of Artificial Intelligence (AI) impact perceived in the general population of the European Union? How are its effects evaluated as regards the job markets? These are just two questions that require, from the general public, an exercise of information and also an exercise of the imagination. A survey is a useful instrument to collect these public perceptions for in-depth analysis of the respective societies. This paper is based on a secondary study of a 2021 Eurobarometer survey that provides insights into the perceived future impact of AI and the social sources of its variability. It reveals evolving aspects about AI’s perception that help us better understand ourselves. The identified trends, relationships, as well as the lack of some expected correlations, offer useful information for various stakeholders interested in the social life of AI.
Publication Title: 2023 24th International Conference on Control Systems and Computer Science (CSCS)

Título: AdaBoost-SVM Face Recognition Algorithm Based on LBP and Gabor Feature Fusion
Autor: X. Lian; C. Liu; C. Gao; G. Ma; Y. Gong; J. Li
Abstract: In recent years, facial recognition technology has seen rapid advancements and is now extensively utilized in security surveillance and financial transactions. The face recognition process usually includes image preprocessing, feature extraction, and classification. However, it is often difficult for a single feature to fully capture the complex features of a human face, thus affecting the accuracy of recognition. To address this issue, this study presented an AdaBoost-SVM face recognition algorithm that fuses local binary pattern and Gabor features, which can enable comprehensive extraction of facial features from images and enhance recognition accuracy. This method first performs bilateral filtering on the face image to reduce noise and retain edge information. Then, it extracts the local binary pattern and Gabor features and weights the fusion. Finally, it uses the AdaBoost-SVM classifier for identification. Moreover, the algorithm performance was comprehensively evaluated using the ten-fold cross-validation method. The experimental results demonstrated that this method achieves recognition accuracy of $\mathbf{9 8. 7 5 \%}$ and $96.25 \%$ on ORL and Yale A face databases respectively, and still performs well in complex lighting and expression-changing environments, significantly improving the accuracy of face recognition and its robustness.
Publication Title: 2024 5th International Conference on Big Data & Artificial Intelligence & Software Engineering (ICBASE)

Título: Harnessing AI Marketing Strategies for the Senior Consumer Market
Autor: J. -H. Lee; J. -H. Son
Abstract: The global population aged 65 and older is projected to more than double—from 700 million in 2020 to approximately 1.5 billion by 2050. As this demographic expands, the silver industry is experiencing rapid growth. In response, there is a growing need to shift perceptions of older adults—from viewing them as passive recipients of care to recognizing them as active, engaged consumers with diverse needs and lifestyles. Artificial intelligence (AI) is emerging as a pivotal driver in this evolving industry, particularly within marketing strategies. Technologies such as voice recognition and the Internet of Things (IoT) are being widely utilized to enhance core aspects of senior life, including health management, mobility, and everyday convenience. These technologies, when effectively integrated into distribution channels and online platforms, can significantly improve seniors' quality of life and enable access to premium lifestyle options.The intersection of AI-driven marketing and the expanding senior consumer base presents both opportunities and challenges. It calls for strategic, data-informed approaches to effectively meet the expectations of this segment. Accordingly, this paper aims to explore seniors' cognitive characteristics, analyze international trends, and examine how AI marketing is being applied in the senior market. Based on these findings, we propose an innovative model tailored to the changing dynamics of the silver market.
Publication Title: 2025 IEEE/ACIS 29th International Conference on Software Engineering, Artificial Intelligence, Networking and Parallel/Distributed Computing (SNPD)

Título: Exploring the Characteristics of the MZ Generation and Their Influence on Kidult
Autor: J. -H. Lee; J. -H. Son
Abstract: The global population aged 65 and older is expected to more than double—from 700 million in 2020 to approximately 1.5 billion by 2050. In response to this demographic shift, the silver industry is rapidly expanding. However, this growing market must also evolve its perception of older adults—seeing them not merely as dependent elderly individuals, but as young-at-heart, active consumers with diverse needs and preferences. Among the technologies most prominently integrated into the senior industry, artificial intelligence (AI) plays a central role in shaping modern marketing strategies. AI-based tools such as voice recognition systems and the Internet of Things (IoT) are increasingly being used to support seniors’ health, mobility, and everyday convenience. In particular, AI enhances the senior consumer experience in distribution and digital platforms, enabling greater comfort, autonomy, and access to a premium lifestyle. The growth of the silver industry and the advancement of AI-driven marketing are closely intertwined. As such, it is critical to develop strategic approaches for effectively applying AI marketing techniques to meet the evolving needs of the senior demographic. Therefore, this paper aims to explore the cognitive and behavioral characteristics of senior consumers, review the current status of senior-related industries across countries, and propose an innovative AI marketing model tailored to the transforming senior market.
Publication Title: 2025 IEEE/ACIS 29th International Conference on Software Engineering, Artificial Intelligence, Networking and Parallel/Distributed Computing (SNPD)

Título: Assessment Software Reliability in Component-Based Software Systems: A Comprehensive Review
Autor: I. A. Tagen; K. T. Wassif; S. Sayed; R. Moawad
Abstract: The reliability of Component-Based Software Systems (CBSS) is a critical factor in the success and longevity of software applications across various domains. This comprehensive review synthesizes methodologies and findings from thirteen key studies, offering a panoramic view of the landscape of CBSS reliability assessment. We explore a spectrum of approaches, from traditional statistical models to advanced computational intelligence techniques, evaluating their effectiveness, challenges, and contexts of applicability. Our review reveals a trend towards the integration of machine learning and fuzzy logic in reliability estimation, reflecting the complexity and dynamic nature of CBSS. Moreover, we identify a significant emphasis on predictive models that cater to early detection of reliability issues, underscoring the proactive aspect of modern software reliability engineering. The findings suggest a need for more adaptable and context-sensitive methods that can accommodate the evolving nature of software components and their interactions. This review not only charts the current state of CBSS reliability assessment but also highlights future directions for research, including the integration of real-time data and the development of more robust models for uncertainty management. Our study serves as a crucial resource for researchers and practitioners seeking to enhance the reliability of CBSS, ultimately contributing to the development of more dependable software systems.
Publication Title: 2024 6th International Conference on Computing and Informatics (ICCI)

Título: A Bibliometric Analysis of AI Applications in Software Development Life Cycle (SDLC)
Autor: K. J. Ooi; M. H. Hoo; C. W. Too; H. C. Beh
Abstract: This study investigates the role of Artificial Intelligence (AI) across phases of the Software Development Life Cycle (SDLC) through a bibliometric analysis, addressing the growing interest and limited consolidated evidence in this domain. Drawing on 599 publications from the Scopus database (2020-2025), the analysis employed VOSviewer and Microsoft Excel to map co-authorship, co-citation, and keyword cooccurrence networks. The findings reveal a significant rise in research output from 2020 to 2025, with emerging themes shifting from foundational AI applications toward generative AI and large language models. However, citation rates lag behind publication volume, and collaborative networks remain largely siloed. The analysis highlights the most influential authors, institutions, and countries, while also identifying gaps such as limited focus on human-centered AI and ethical implications. It is recommended that future research broaden the scope of collaboration and explore these underrepresented themes to better support responsible AI integration in software development.
Publication Title: 2025 6th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Data Sciences (AiDAS)

Título: From Tea Leaves to System Maps: A Survey and Framework on Context-Aware Machine Learning Monitoring
Autor: J. Leest; C. Raibulet; P. Lago; I. Gerostathopoulos
Abstract: Machine learning (ML) models in production fail when their broader systems – from data pipelines to deployment environments – deviate from training assumptions, not merely due to statistical anomalies in input data. Despite extensive work on data drift, data validation, and out-of-distribution detection, ML monitoring research remains largely model-centric while neglecting contextual information: auxiliary signals about the system around the model (external factors, data pipelines, downstream applications). Incorporating this context turns statistical anomalies into actionable alerts and structured root-cause analysis. Drawing on a systematic review of 94 primary studies, we identify three dimensions of contextual information for ML monitoring: the system element concerned (natural environment or technical infrastructure); the aspect of that element (runtime states, structural relationships, prescriptive properties); and the representation used (formal constructs or informal formats). This forms the Contextual System-Aspect-Representation (C-SAR) framework, a descriptive model synthesizing our findings. We identify 20 recurring triplets across these dimensions and map them to the monitoring activities they support. This study provides a holistic perspective on ML monitoring: from interpreting “tea leaves” (i.e., isolated data and performance statistics) to constructing and managing “system maps” (i.e., end-to-end views that connect data, models, and operating context).
Publication Title: IEEE Transactions on Software Engineering

Título: Trustworthy and Robust AI Deployment by Design: A framework to inject best practice support into AI deployment pipelines
Autor: A. Schmelczer; J. Visser
Abstract: Trustworthy and robust deployment of AI applications requires adherence to a range of AI engineering best practices. But, while professionals already have access to frameworks for deploying AI, case studies and developer surveys have found that many deployments do not follow best practices.We hypothesize that the adoption of AI deployment best practices can be improved by finding less complex framework designs that combine ease of use with built-in support for best practices. To investigate this hypothesis, we applied a design science approach to develop a new framework, called GreatAI, and evaluate its ease of use and best practice support.The initial design focusses on the domain of natural language processing (NLP), but with generalisation in mind. To assess applicability and generalisability, we conducted interviews with ten practitioners. We also assessed best practice coverage.We found that our framework helps implement 33 best practices through an accessible interface. These target the transition from prototype to production phase in the AI development lifecycle. Feedback from professional data scientists and software engineers showed that ease of use and functionality are equally important in deciding to adopt deployment technologies, and the proposed framework was rated positively in both dimensions.
Publication Title: 2023 IEEE/ACM 2nd International Conference on AI Engineering – Software Engineering for AI (CAIN)

Título: Architectural Styles and Quality Attributes in AI-Based Video Software: A Systematic Literature Review
Autor: M. H. Alshahrani; M. S. Maashi; A. B. Najjar
Abstract: AI-based video software systems are increasingly prevalent in surveillance, content generation, and multimedia analytics domains. However, we know little about these systems’ architectures or how they address software-level quality attributes. This gap in documentation limits the reproducibility of such systems. This paper addresses this issue and presents a Systematic Literature Review (SLR) of 139 peer-reviewed studies published between 2020 and 2025. It investigates which software architecture styles they report, the reasons behind their selection, and the quality attributes evaluated in developing AI-based video systems. The results show that only eight studies ( $\approx 6$ %) mention a software architecture style—primarily client-server, component-based, cloud-based, and serverless—and only 10 ( $\approx 7$ %) explicitly discuss software quality attributes, such as efficiency, complexity, robustness, performance, and scalability. The findings highlight a significant literature gap in architectural and quality-related reporting. This review contributes new insights into underexplored aspects of AI-based video systems by elucidating the interplay between architecture decisions and system-level quality outcomes. It emphasizes the need for more systematic documentation of software engineering decisions to support reliable, maintainable, and scalable AI applications.
Publication Title: IEEE Access

Título: The European way of doing Artificial Intelligence: The state of play implementing Trustworthy AI
Autor: B. Beckert
Abstract: “Trustworthy AI” is the concept of the European Commission to facilitate acceptance and diffusion of Artificial Intelligence in Europe. The concept claims that European AI applications shall be lawful, ethical and robust, both from a technical and societal perspective. The contribution asks for the state of play of implementing the concept of Trustworthy AI. More concretely, it sets out to identify concrete cases of implementing Trustworthy AI in order to analyse approaches and experiences. However, it turns out that such projects currently only exist in a research context and at neither large companies nor start-ups or medium-sized companies provide suitable examples, with only a few exceptions. This gives rise to the question, why companies today ignore or even avoid the carefully worked out guidelines to implement Trustworthy AI. Three answers are given which refer to time-to-market considerations, different mindsets of software engineers and social scientists, and the fact that implementing Trustworthy AI requires of firms to go the extra mile with additional expertise and governance structures. Following this, two possibilities are presented to increase in the number of companies actually picking up on the guidelines and concretely implementing Trustworthy AI. These possibilities are firstly to break down existing implementation guidelines to the requirements of software engineers, computer scientists and managers, and secondly to embed social scientists and stakeholders in the implementation process.
Publication Title: 2021 60th FITCE Communication Days Congress for ICT Professionals: Industrial Data – Cloud, Low Latency and Privacy (FITCE)

Título: Data Structures for Routing Table of the Distributed Key-Value Store Based on Order Preserving Linear Hashing and Skip Graph with the Load Balancing Method
Autor: K. Higuchi; A. Miyazaki; K. Hasegawa; T. Tsuji
Abstract: In this paper, data structures for reducing redundancy of routing information on the distributed key-value store based on order preserving linear hashing and Skip Graph with the load balancing method are proposed and evaluated. In this system, data are divided by order preserving linear hashing and Skip Graph is used for overlay network. For load balancing, by storing many Skip Graph nodes in one physical node, any highest-load Skip Graph can be divided. By this method, load balancing can be done. But the number of Skip Graph nodes becomes very many and redundant routing information exists. But this redundant routing information has regularity. In this paper, two types of data structure for reducing redundant routing elements are proposed. They are tree structure and array structure. By calculating space for them and searching time, the proposed data structures are experimentally evaluated.
Publication Title: 2022 23rd ACIS International Summer Virtual Conference on Software Engineering, Artificial Intelligence, Networking and Parallel/Distributed Computing (SNPD-Summer)

Título: Harnessing the Power of AI in RD&E Management: The Innovation Index
Autor: F. Platania; C. Toscano Hernandez; I. E. Ouadghiri; J. Peillex
Abstract: This article investigates the complex relationship between artificial intelligence (AI)-related technological innovation and its impact on the market returns of leading technology companies. We introduce the “Innovation Index,” a latent variable based on the number of AI-related patents, and incorporate it into the traditional Fama–French model to better capture the influence of AI advancements on financial performance. Using a state-space representation, our empirical analysis suggests that AI-related innovation significantly contributes to the market returns of prominent and leading technology firms. The findings highlight the strategic importance of AI investments in sustaining long-term growth and competitiveness.
Publication Title: IEEE Transactions on Engineering Management

Título: AI-Driven Continuous Integration and Continuous Deployment in Software Engineering
Autor: A. S. Mohammed; V. R. Saddi; S. K. Gopal; S. Dhanasekaran; M. S. Naruka
Abstract: AI driven Continuous Integration and Continuous Deployment is a new way of managing and continually updating a software project. This process, powered by Artificial Intelligence, automates the entire software delivery and deployment process - from code submission to monitoring and bug fixing. It eliminates manual errors and allows for multiple versions to be tested in parallel, saving time and effort. By increasing agility, it allows organizations to launch new features to production faster than ever. Continuous Integration and Continuous Deployment leverages artificial intelligence in implementation and execution. It automates the process of integration, testing, packaging and deployment. Furthermore, AI is used to detect and fix bugs which can prevent delays and costly production bugs. AI driven Continuous Integration and Continuous Deployment has become an increasingly popular development strategy. It helps reduce the overall cost and accelerate the software's production cycles, making it easier for developers to quickly get their features and services in the hands of the market.
Publication Title: 2024 2nd International Conference on Disruptive Technologies (ICDT)

Título: Toward Pattern-Oriented Machine Learning Reliability Argumentation
Autor: T. Ayukawa; J. H. Husen; N. Yoshioka; H. Washizaki; N. Ubayashi
Abstract: Machine learning systems must be reliable. The Goal Structure Notation and Machine Learning Reliability Solution Patterns are often utilized to demonstrate the reliability of machine learning systems. However, there are instances where engineers fail to recognize the possibility of utilizing patterns because they lack prior knowledge and are not machine learning experts. To overcome these challenges, herein we propose the Pattern Application Support Framework. This framework should guide engineers and facilitate accurate pattern applications as it complements the lack of prior knowledge and guides engineers when applying Machine Learning Reliability Solution Patterns.
Publication Title: 2024 IEEE 35th International Symposium on Software Reliability Engineering Workshops (ISSREW)

Título: Research on the Application of Artificial Intelligence in Cybersecurity: Integrating Advanced Technologies to Improve Threat Detection and Response
Autor: T.S.Balaji; R.Nadanasabai; R. J. Ravikumar; S.Sharanyaa; R.Meenal; N. P. Deepthi
Abstract: In the current age of digital change, the field of cybersecurity is faced with progressively advanced and enduring dangers. Conventional security measures are finding it difficult to keep up with the fast-changing nature of cyber threats. Artificial intelligence (AI) offers a significant chance to enhance threat detection and response systems by leveraging its sophisticated data processing and pattern recognition abilities. This study explores the incorporation of artificial intelligence (AI) technology in the field of cybersecurity, specifically aiming to improve the effectiveness of systems that identify and respond to threats. The paper commences by examining the present difficulties encountered by cybersecurity experts, which encompass the large number and diverse range of potential threat vectors, the covert and flexible nature of contemporary malware, and the limitations imposed by resource constraints on the efficacy of human-driven analysis. The text explores the current cutting-edge AI strategies used to tackle these difficulties, including anomaly identification through machine learning algorithms, predictive analysis using deep learning models, and threat intelligence collecting through natural language processing. The research also examines the distinct factors of AI in the cybersecurity field, such as the requirement for immediate decision-making, the significance of comprehending the model, and the possibility of hostile attacks on AI systems. This study seeks to analyse current literature and emerging trends to gain a deep understanding of how artificial intelligence (AI) can enhance cybersecurity. Additionally, it aims to identify the obstacles and deficiencies that must be overcome to effectively incorporate AI technologies into this crucial domain. To summarise, the research highlights the considerable capacity of AI to transform cybersecurity practices, providing a way to develop more robust and flexible defence methods against the numerous dangers that exist in the digital realm.
Publication Title: 2024 5th IEEE Global Conference for Advancement in Technology (GCAT)

Título: Benchmarking Machine Learning Solutions in Production
Autor: L. Cardoso Silva; F. Rezende Zagatti; B. Silva Sette; L. Nildaimon dos Santos Silva; D. Lucrédio; D. Furtado Silva; H. de Medeiros Caseli
Abstract: Machine learning (ML) is becoming critical to many businesses. Keeping an ML solution online and responding is therefore a necessity, and is part of the MLOps (Machine Learning operationalization) movement. One aspect for this process is monitoring not only prediction quality, but also system resources. This is important to correctly provide the necessary infrastructure, either using a fully-managed cloud platform or a local solution. This is not a difficult task, as there are many tools available. However, it requires some planning and knowledge about what to monitor. Also, many ML professionals are not experts in system operations and may not have the skills to easily setup a monitoring and benchmarking environment. In the spirit of MLOps, this paper presents an approach, based on a simple API and set of tools, to monitor ML solutions. The approach was tested with 9 different solutions. The results indicate that the approach can deliver useful information to help in decision making, proper resource provision and operation of ML systems.
Publication Title: 2020 19th IEEE International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)

Título: Implementation of Temporal Action Detection Workflow for Video Data Analysis with the Use of Machine Learning Operations
Autor: M. S. Kupriyanov; Y. A. Shichkina; S. E. Ilin
Abstract: The article aims to discuss the peculiarities of using the Machine Learning Operations paradigm in the context of addressing the task of temporal action detection in video data. The results of applying Machine Learning Operations to the aforementioned task are analyzed at three levels of machine learning workflow, namely, at the level of machine learning system design, at the level of machine learning system components, and at the level of elements within the machine learning system components. It is emphasized that Machine Learning Operations facilitates the procedures of designing, developing and deploying the temporal action detection workflow.
Publication Title: 2024 V International Conference on Neural Networks and Neurotechnologies (NeuroNT)

Título: Towards Software Engineering Perspective for BDI Agent
Autor: S. Y. Wai; W. S. Cheah; S. K. Wai; M. A. Khairuddin
Abstract: BDI agent is a cognitive architecture to model human reasoning based on a philosophical stream. Based on the survey, the BDI agent is widely adopted in agent based social simulation to model a complex and realistic human behaviour. To support BDI agent development, Prometheus and tactic development framework (extended Prometheus are introduced. Although Prometheus and TDF are introduced to model BDI agent, the modelling and simulating BDI agent is not a straightforward process. As simulation is one of the pillars in IR4.0, it is undoubtedly that there will be a higher demand and usage of agent simulation in real world problem. Hence, there is a need to call for software engineering perspective for BDI agent. Inline with the works on Software Engineering for AI application development, this paper introduces an alternative method to model BDI agent through a software engineering perspective. The requirement model, design and implementation of BDI based on software engineering approach are covered in this paper. A walkthrough example of BDI based a bush fire evacuation simulation has revealed the potential usage of the method.
Publication Title: 2021 4th International Symposium on Agents, Multi-Agent Systems and Robotics (ISAMSR)

Título: Introducing MLOps to Facilitate the Development of Machine Learning Models in Agronomy: A Case Study
Autor: D. Ruggeri; G. Tazza; L. Vidács
Abstract: While machine learning (ML) and deep learning (DL) are increasingly being adopted in agronomy, the literature shows that the use of ML Operations (MLOps) frameworks remains scarce during the research stage. This study discusses the utility of MLOps in enhancing the reproducibility and transparency of ML projects even before deployment in production. Moreover, by showing the process of integrating these practices in a pre-existing codebase from an ML study in agronomy, this work showcases the transformative potential of MLOps and serves as a guideline for efficiently utilizing these frameworks. Among the many MLOps frameworks, ClearML was selected and integrated into all the project’s aspects, from data processing through hyper-parameter optimization to orchestrating the entire workflow. This integration not only streamlined dataset management, experiment tracking, and hyper-parameter optimization but also demonstrated the utility of a more structured approach to ML project design, leading to more informed design choices. Our findings underscore the potential of these practices to revolutionize the reproducibility, transparency, and design of ML projects in computational agronomy research.
Publication Title: IEEE Access

Título: Mlops as a Service for AI-Native 6G Networks
Autor: S. Kukliński; R. Kołakowski; B. Mastej
Abstract: This paper concerns the lifecycle and runtime management of AI components of AI-native networks, in which the AI components are deeply integrated with network functions. The paper presents an overview of the standardised, IT-based, and research approaches to ML pipeline management. To address the identified gaps, we introduce the automated MLOps as a Service concept in which an ML model deployed in the network is continuously monitored for data and concept drifts, as well as network performance degradation, and on that basis, specific ML pipeline operations are triggered to improve the model's operation. The proposed approach allows for selecting local (inplace) or remote training by a network operator or external ML service providers. The benefits of selecting different training options are demonstrated in the AI-based network analytics scenario.
Publication Title: 2025 IEEE 11th International Conference on Network Softwarization (NetSoft)

Título: Wearable Parkinson’s Disease Finger Tapping Quantitative Evaluation Algorithm Combined with Impedance Sensing
Autor: J. -S. Fong; Y. -H. Chuang; F. -S. Yu; I. -C. Wey; S. F. Wang
Abstract: This paper proposes an Artificial Intelligence (AI) identification algorithm that combined the human body resistance and capacitance sensing. The measured human body impedance data is analyzed by a simple four-arithmetic algorithm, and then four different AI algorithms are used to determine whether or not according to the characteristics of Parkinson’s Disease (PD) patients. The algorithm of this paper is based on the impedance data of normal people and PD patients through the calculation circuit proposed in this paper to analyze the difference in body resistance, the number of finger fits, finger kneading cycles, and finger kneading amplitude to accurately distinguish the fingers of PD patients Symptoms of tremor and stiffness. Through the feature analysis of four AI algorithms, it is judged that the accuracy rate of PD patients is higher than 90%.
Publication Title: 2021 IEEE/ACIS 22nd International Conference on Software Engineering, Artificial Intelligence, Networking and Parallel/Distributed Computing (SNPD)

Título: Towards Concrete and Connected AI Risk Assessment (C2AIRA): A Systematic Mapping Study
Autor: B. Xia; Q. Lu; H. Perera; L. Zhu; Z. Xing; Y. Liu; J. Whittle
Abstract: The rapid development of artificial intelligence (AI) has led to increasing concerns about the capability of AI systems to make decisions and behave responsibly. Responsible AI (RAI) refers to the development and use of AI systems that benefit humans, society, and the environment while minimising the risk of negative consequences. To ensure responsible AI, the risks associated with AI systems' development and use must be identified, assessed and mitigated. Various AI risk assessment frameworks have been released recently by governments, organisations, and companies. However, it can be challenging for AI stakeholders to have a clear picture of the available frameworks and determine the most suitable ones for a specific context. Additionally, there is a need to identify areas that require further research or development of new frameworks, as well as updating and maintaining existing ones. To fill the gap, we present a mapping study of 16 existing AI risk assessment frameworks from the industry, governments, and non-government organizations (NGOs). We identify key characteristics of each framework and analyse them in terms of RAI principles, stakeholders, system lifecycle stages, geographical locations, targeted domains, and assessment methods. Our study provides a comprehensive analysis of the current state of the frameworks and highlights areas of convergence and divergence among them. We also identify the deficiencies in existing frameworks and outlines the essential characteristics of a concrete and connected framework AI risk assessment (C2 AIRA) framework. Our findings and insights can help relevant stakeholders choose suitable AI risk assessment frameworks and guide the design of future frameworks towards concreteness and connectedness.
Publication Title: 2023 IEEE/ACM 2nd International Conference on AI Engineering – Software Engineering for AI (CAIN)

Título: Utilizing Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) to Identify Determinants of Coffee Quality
Autor: K. Sermmany; P. Wanjantuk; W. Leelapatra
Abstract: This paper explores the transformative potential of Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) in the context of coffee quality assessment, an area traditionally governed by subjective evaluation. By applying machine learning models, specifically a Random Forest Classifier enhanced by SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations) values, we identified crucial determinants of coffee quality, such as Category Two defects and high-altitude growth conditions. Our study demonstrates that machine learning can not only match but potentially exceed the accuracy of human experts in predicting coffee quality. More importantly, XAI has provided these models with a layer of transparency, making their complex predictions accessible and actionable for stakeholders in the coffee industry. This integration of AI into coffee quality assessment promises to standardize and optimize the evaluation process, offering a reliable guide for improving practices across the production chain. The findings underscore the broader impact of AI in agriculture, suggesting that such technology could be a harbinger of increased efficiency, sustainability, and trust in food production systems worldwide.
Publication Title: 2024 21st International Joint Conference on Computer Science and Software Engineering (JCSSE)

Título: Multi-Level XAI-Driven MLOps Pipeline for the Adjustment of Fruit and Vegetable Classifiers
Autor: F. J. Iriarte; M. E. Ortiz; L. Unzueta; J. Martínez; J. Zaldivar; I. Arganda-Carreras
Abstract: In this paper, we present a machine learning operations (MLOps) pipeline that exploits explainable artificial intelligence (XAI) to adjust deep neural network (DNN)-based fruit and vegetable image classifiers to data observed at super-market self-checkouts. DNNs are currently the most successful AI models for several automation tasks, including fruit and vegetable classification. However, adjusting them to on-site observations to avoid model drift is challenging, as they work as black boxes that take inputs and return results without showing the processes that lead to their responses. State-of-the-art XAI techniques could help mitigate this problem, but integrating them in MLOps pipelines is also challenging due to their high computational cost. We propose multi-task B-cos (MTBC) DNNs integrated at different pipeline levels to obtain automated information about the system's on-site performance. MTBC DNNs allow indentification of fruit and vegetable types with contextual attributes in real time and generation of contribution maps that highlight task-relevant features for enhanced model adjustment. Experimental results show that MTBC DNNs obtain similar accuracy and performance to the equivalent baseline DNNs for fruit and vegetable classification but with the added benefit of explainability, supporting MLOps processes such as drift detection and exploratory data analysis.
Publication Title: 2024 IEEE 12th International Conference on Intelligent Systems (IS)

Título: Exploratory Analysis of Software Engineering Skill in Thailand's Software Industry Using NLP Techniques
Autor: N. Phakdee; S. Pradubnak; A. Siraphatwongkorn
Abstract: The COVID-19 pandemic has contributed to digital transformation across industries, with the software sector in Thailand experiencing rapid growth. This research aims to identify the essential technical and soft skills required by the Thai software industry using natural language processing (NLP). The analysis shows that programming languages such as SQL, Java, and Python, as well as development tools such as Docker and Git, are essential for all roles. At the same time, soft skills such as communication, problem-solving, and analytical remain important. The findings highlight a significant skills gap that is depriving university graduates of meaningful career opportunities, emphasizing the need for an overhaul of higher education course content. This study gives empirical evidence to support curriculum redesign and workforce development policies so that future graduates are skillfully suited to Thailand's digital economy and the changing needs of Industry 5.0.
Publication Title: 2025 IEEE/ACIS 29th International Conference on Software Engineering, Artificial Intelligence, Networking and Parallel/Distributed Computing (SNPD)

Título: Practices for Managing Machine Learning Products: A Multivocal Literature Review
Autor: I. Alves; L. A. F. Leite; P. Meirelles; F. Kon; C. S. R. Aguiar
Abstract: Machine learning (ML) has grown in popularity in the software industry due to its ability to solve complex problems. Developing ML systems involves more uncertainty and risk because it requires identifying a business opportunity and managing source code, data, and trained models. Our research aims to identify the existing practices used in the industry for building ML applications and comprehending the organizational complexity of adopting ML systems. We conducted a multivocal literature review and then created a taxonomy of the practices applied to the ML system life cycle discussed among practitioners and researchers. The core of the study emerged from 41 selected posts from the grey literature and 37 selected scientific papers. Applying Initial Coding and Focused Coding techniques into these data, we mapped 91 practices into six core categories related to designing, developing, testing, and deploying ML systems. The results, including a taxonomy of practices, provide organizations with valuable insights to identify gaps in their current ML processes and practices and a roadmap for improving, optimizing, and managing ML systems.
Publication Title: IEEE Transactions on Engineering Management

Título: A mesh quality discrimination method based on convolutional neural network
Autor: Z. Xu; X. Chen; L. Chi; J. Liu; C. Gong
Abstract: With the rapid development of highperformance computing, computational fluid dynamics (CFD) has become an important part of hydrodynamics and aerodynamics. Mesh quality is the key factor that affects the accuracy and efficiency of CFD numerical calculation. However, the current the process of mesh quality discrimination is very time-consuming. The manpower time needed for this process takes up a large proportion in the whole numerical calculation process. A large number of artificial intelligence algorithms have been put forward to replace the human to efficiently complete all kinds of tedious tasks. In this paper, we propose a convolutional neural network (CNN) based mesh quality discrimination method, MeshNet. MeshNet uses residual neural network structure to learn mesh features and automatically judge the mesh quality. The experimental results show that the proposed network can greatly save labor time cost and achieve an accuracy of 94.41% for mesh quality discrimination.
Publication Title: 2020 IEEE International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Computer Applications (ICAICA)

Título: Analysis and Reflections on the Use of Artificial Intelligence in Teaching Management and Evaluation - Analysis Based on CiteSpace
Autor: L. Guo; B. Wen
Abstract: In the era of smart education, artificial intelligence provides a new possibility for “smart education” and “smart classroom.” In order that understands the development of AI in teaching management and evaluation, this paper uses bibliometric and content analysis methods, combined with the knowledge graph visualization software Citespace, to comb through the literature on AI in teaching management and evaluation from 1991 to 2023, and use citespace analysis software to analyze the literature from keyword-based co-occurrence mapping, clustering mapping, timeline mapping, and other mappings to analyze the research hotspots and characteristics of AI use in instructional management and evaluation. Finally, we propose reflections based on this research problem and draw conclusions and prospects for the use of AI in teaching management and assessment: (1) to enrich teaching tools and teaching resources. (2) to improve teaching efficiency and quality and help teachers get away from tedious teaching affairs and devote themselves to teaching activities that promote student learning. (3) to change teaching methods. (4) to achieve educational equity.
Publication Title: 2023 IEEE 14th International Conference on Software Engineering and Service Science (ICSESS)

Título: Application of Artificial Intelligence Methods for Generating Mathematical Problems in Primary Education: Analysis of Effectiveness and Prospects for Automated Assessment
Autor: N. R. Salavatovich; A. E. Yurievna
Abstract: The present study aims to evaluate the effectiveness of using modern neural network models (ChatGPT 4o, Claude 3.5 Sonnet, GigaChat 3.0, YandexGPT 3) for generating mathematical problems for elementary school students. The research conducts a comparative analysis of the quality of generated problems, assesses the possibility of their direct use without expert review, and develops recommendations for automating the quality control process. The methodology includes the generation of various types of mathematical problems, their expert evaluation according to developed criteria, and statistical analysis of the results. The study identifies the strengths and weaknesses of each model, determines the types of problems in which the models demonstrate the best results, and suggests directions for further improvement of AI systems in the educational context.
Publication Title: 2024 7th International Conference on Machine Learning and Natural Language Processing (MLNLP)

Título: Cloud-Client Integrated Designed Financial Robot Based on Artificial Intelligence
Autor: S. Shi; G. Li; B. Han
Abstract: Financial robot is an important means to realize the automation of financial process. Considering that there is still room for expansion in the current research on financial robots, this paper proposes a financial robot based on cloud integrated design and big data analysis, and designs the financial robot on the end side, including a financial document identification module based on deep neural network, a dialogue assistance module based on natural language processing, and an error correction check module based on cloud data; Build the financial sharing cloud on the cloud side, including the financial processing system based on database and the financial big data intelligent analysis module based on data mining. Based on the cloud integrated financial robot architecture, an intelligent reimbursement process is proposed to provide simplified services for the enterprise's accounting processing process and provide reference analysis of the financial situation for the enterprise's decision-makers.
Publication Title: 2023 7th International Conference on Management Engineering, Software Engineering and Service Sciences (ICMSS)

Título: On-Device AI for Secure Patient Health Monitoring
Autor: A. Rangi; Y. -T. Song
Abstract: The increasing adoption of artificial intelligence (AI) in mobile health applications has introduced critical concerns regarding user privacy and data security. Privacy risks, particularly when health data is transmitted to cloud servers, present a major barrier to widespread adoption of AI-driven personal health monitoring systems. Regulatory frameworks such as HIPAA and GDPR outline strict requirements for securing patient data, yet achieving compliance in mobile environments remains challenging. Previous research has explored encryption techniques and secure data transmission methods to address privacy concerns. While effective to some extent, these methods still rely on cloud infrastructure, creating vulnerabilities and limiting offline functionality. Lightweight on-device AI models have emerged as a promising alternative, but ensuring privacy without compromising diagnostic accuracy presents a technical challenge. In this paper, we propose an on-device AI model that integrates TensorFlow Lite for lightweight inference and the Gemma 2B language model for personalized health insights. Our model addresses key privacy concerns by analyzing health data locally on the user’s device, ensuring secure, private assessments without relying on cloud connectivity. We implemented an on-device AI module for patient health monitoring as a proof of concept.
Publication Title: 2025 IEEE/ACIS 23rd International Conference on Software Engineering Research, Management and Applications (SERA)

Título: Spine X-ray Image Segmentation Based on Transformer and Adaptive Optimized Postprocessing
Autor: L. Zhang; J. Yang; D. Liu; F. Zhang; S. Nie; Y. Tan; T. Guo
Abstract: Accurate segmentation of vertebral blocks in X-ray images of the whole spine is necessary for intelligent diagnosis of spinal diseases. However, the vertebral block in X-ray spine image has similar appearance with background, which cause a huge challenge to segmentation performance. Even though the classical Unet network and existing popular methods have been proved as effective approaches in medical image segmentation, they still create some conglutination between vertebral blocks in spine X-ray images. To overcome this problem, we propose a novel spine X-ray image segmentation method based on transformer and adaptive optimized postprocessing to improve segmentation performance. Firstly, we adopt the Segmenter network based on Transformer framework to obtain initial result. Then, an adaptive post-processing optimization strategy is proposed to optimize segmentation results. Finally, experiments on AASCE 2019 challenge dataset show that our model can gain best performance compared with existing methods.
Publication Title: 2022 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Software Engineering and Artificial Intelligence (SEAI)

Título: Research of Scratch Programming Recommendation System Based on MED and Knowledge Graph
Autor: Y. -t. He; B. -J. Guo; J. Lu; Y. -p. Xu; M. Gong
Abstract: In recent years, children's programming education and primary and secondary school programming education are gradually gaining importance. After the State Council issued relevant documents on the development of artificial intelligence, the status of programming education for primary and secondary schools has been improved. At the same time, in order to improve learning efficiency, it is especially important to build a personalized programming topic recommendation system that takes into account the differences in learning ability and learning characteristics of different learners. This paper introduces a knowledge graph based on the traditional recommendation method, divides a single program block in Scratch programming into several knowledge points that are related to each other, processes the strings and calculates the editing distances according to the topics, finds out the similarities between the topics, combines the difficulty of the topics and knowledge points, and then designs a personalized recommendation system for Scratch programming topics based on the editing distances and knowledge graphs based on the learners' historical answer data, which effectively improves the recommendation accuracy.
Publication Title: 2020 5th International Conference on Mechanical, Control and Computer Engineering (ICMCCE)

Título: AI Ethics Principles in Practice: Perspectives of Designers and Developers
Autor: C. Sanderson; D. Douglas; Q. Lu; E. Schleiger; J. Whittle; J. Lacey; G. Newnham; S. Hajkowicz; C. Robinson; D. Hansen
Abstract: As consensus across the various published AI ethics principles is approached, a gap remains between high-level principles and practical techniques that can be readily adopted to design and develop responsible AI systems. We examine the practices and experiences of researchers and engineers from Australia’s national scientific research agency (CSIRO), who are involved in designing and developing AI systems for many application areas. Semi-structured interviews were used to examine how the practices of the participants relate to and align with a set of high-level AI ethics principles proposed by the Australian Government. The principles comprise: (1) privacy protection and security, (2) reliability and safety, (3) transparency and explainability, (4) fairness, (5) contestability, (6) accountability, (7) human-centred values, (8) human, social and environmental well-being. Discussions on the gained insights from the interviews include various tensions and trade-offs between the principles, and provide suggestions for implementing each high-level principle. We also present suggestions aiming to enhance associated support mechanisms.
Publication Title: IEEE Transactions on Technology and Society

Título: Skeleton-ID: AI-driven Human Identification
Autor: A. Valsecchi; O. Gómez; A. González; M. Macías; M. De Dios; M. Panizo; K. Prada; M. Flores; S. Kaiser; N. Lurromi; E. Bermejo; P. Mesejo; S. Damas; O. Cordón; O. Ibáñez
Abstract: Victims of crime, migration, natural disasters, and armed conflicts often remain unidentified due to the absence of DNA samples and fingerprints. Skeleton-based identification (ID) methods could help to identify these victims because they are suitable for poorly preserved bodies and require comparison data that is relatively easy to obtain —e.g., a simple photo of the victim— instead of a DNA sample of a close relative or access to a database in another country. Skeleton-ID™ is a game-changing software designed for skeleton-based human identification using artificial intelligence (AI). Combining forensic anthropology and odontology methods with cutting-edge image processing and AI technologies allows us to apply these methods on a large scale. This leads to very high reliability (up to 99%), obtaining explainable results, and a drastic reduction of identification time (days to minutes). The software is accessible through a web browser and can process ante-mortem (AM) information on missing persons as well as post-mortem (PM) information. Usable data includes ordinary photos, 3D scans of bones, x-rays, and dental records.
Publication Title: 2023 IEEE Conference on Artificial Intelligence (CAI)

Título: Preliminary Literature Review of Machine Learning System Development Practices
Autor: Y. Watanabe; H. Washizaki; K. Sakamoto; D. Saito; K. Honda; N. Tsuda; Y. Fukazawa; N. Yoshioka
Abstract: To guide practitioners and researchers to design and research Machine Learning (ML) system development processes, we conduct a preliminary literature review on ML system development practices. We identified seven papers and two other papers determined in an ad-hoc review. Our findings include emphasized phases in ML system developments, frequently described ML-specific practices, and tailored traditional practices.
Publication Title: 2021 IEEE 45th Annual Computers, Software, and Applications Conference (COMPSAC)

Título: Homomorphic Encryption for AI-Based Applications: Challenges and Opportunities
Autor: R. Hamza
Abstract: Homomorphic encryption is a technique that allows computations with encrypted data without revealing the data or requiring decryption. This technique has many potential applications in artificial intelligence (AI), where privacy and security are critical. However, implementing homomorphic encryption for AI also presents many challenges and opportunities from a software engineering perspective. In this paper, we provide a comprehensive overview of the current state of the art in homomorphic encryption for AI-based applications and discuss some important software engineering aspects of implementing homomorphic encryption solutions for AI scenarios. We review recent research challenges in applying homomorphic encryption to AI-based applications and discuss their software engineering aspects. We also compare different homomorphic encryption algorithms and libraries in terms of their security, performance, usability, interoperability, functionality, and scalability. Finally, we highlight some open challenges and future directions for research and development in this area.
Publication Title: 2023 15th International Conference on Knowledge and Systems Engineering (KSE)

Título: Digital Sovereignty and Software Engineering for the IoT-laden, AI/ML-driven Era
Autor: C. Berger
Abstract: Today’s software engineering already needs to deal with challenges originating from the multidisciplinarity that is required to realize IoT products. Many variants consist of sensor/actuator-powered systems that nowadays use AI/ML systems to better cope with the unstructuredness of their intended operational design domain (ODD). At the same time, though, such systems need to be monitored, diagnosed, maintained, and evolved using cloud-powered dashboards and data analytics pipelines that process, aggregate, and analyze countless data points–preferably in real-time. This position paper discusses selected aspects related to Digital Sovereignty from a software engineering’s perspective for the IoT-laden, AI/ML-driven era. While we can undeniably expect more and more benefits from such solutions, light shall be shed in particular on challenges and responsibilities at design-and operation-time that, at minimum, prepare for and enable or, even better, preserve and extend digital sovereignty from a software engineering’s perspective.
Publication Title: 2022 IEEE International Conference on Services Computing (SCC)

Título: Scalable Multi-Model Orchestration in AI Microservices with Kubernetes and Serverless for Event-Driven MLOps Pipelines
Autor: V. T. Ponnaganti
Abstract: The ability to efficiently orchestrate multiple machine learning models will be crucial in this ever-changing landscape of AI for deploying scalable and resilient applications. This paper presents a holistic framework for scalable multi-model orchestration within the architecture of AI microservices-a Kubernetes and serverless-driven, event-driven pipeline in MLOps. The proposed framework integrates Kubernetes, which manages container orchestration with robust scalability, automated deployment, and seamless management of microservices. For this, serverless computing supports dealing with on-demand resource allocation, less operational overhead, and cost-effectiveness. This pipeline is further optimized by an event-driven paradigm, where events generated from real-time data streams trigger the model workflows to assure responses in time and relevance. We will further demonstrate the applicability of the proposed framework on a publicly available dataset, CIFAR-10, and show how diverse models for image classification can be orchestrated on one platform to performant and reliable results in real-world scenarios. These experiments yield a reasonable improvement in the speed, scalability, and operational efficiency at deployment, proving the potential of combining Kubernetes and serverless for advanced MLOps implementations.
Publication Title: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)

Título: Federated Learning Operations (FLOps): Challenges, Lifecycle and Approaches
Autor: Q. Cheng; G. Long
Abstract: Federated Learning has witnessed a rapid growth in research and industry applications as it offers the benefits of privacy preserving while contributing to the global model training. Cross-silo federated learning systems which are usually geographically distributed and cross-organizational are becoming a reality. Although DevOps and MLOps methodologies may help improving traditional machine learning systems' development efficiency and productivity, it is still challenging for them to develop cross-silo federated learning systems in a productive way. In this paper, we propose FLOps (Federated Learning Operations), a new methodology for developing cross-silo federated learning systems efficiently and continuously. By elaborating the challenges that FLOps is facing, we construct the lifecycle of FLOps, and propose approaches to FLOps. Finally, we highlight potential research directions of FLOps.
Publication Title: 2022 International Conference on Technologies and Applications of Artificial Intelligence (TAAI)

Título: A Higher Education Information Management System Based on Digital Twin Technology and Artificial Intelligence Algorithms
Autor: Y. Ye
Abstract: With the rapid development of modern information technology and the continuous advancement of school education reform, the development of school education management information has become the inevitable trend of education management reform and development in the information age. With the advent of the Internet of everything world, the new generation of information technology and entity are deeply integrated, as an important frontier of information technology “digital twin technology” has become the focus. Digital twin technology refers to the use of information technologies such as the Internet of Things, big data, artificial intelligence and virtual reality to fully realize the digital expression of entities in the virtual world and form an information ecosystem of close-loop operation and intelligent decision-making with entities. In this paper, based on the actual development of colleges and universities, relying on the “big data basis” and “modeling ecosystem” of digital twin technology, combined with artificial intelligence algorithm, the digital twin technology is innovatively applied in the informatization of college education management. On the basis of multi-dimensional and multi-modal data collection and mining of college education management, a set of digital twin model of university education management informatization is constructed, and the model is applied in scene.
